items.title: Discontinue codesigning and notarization on macOS
items.author: lemmy
items.description:
**Expired Keys/Certificates**

```
notBefore=Apr 17 17:57:36 2020 GMT
notAfter=Apr 18 17:57:36 2025 GMT
subject=UID = ***, CN = Developer ID Application: M K (***), OU = ***, O = M K, C = DE
```

The code-signing certificate has expired.

In addition, my Apple Developer membership will expire soon. As a result, code signing and notarization will be temporarily unavailable. Going forward, macOS users will need to manually whitelist the TLA+ Toolbox to run it. Alternatively, it is recommended to migrate to one of the Toolbox’s successors.

items.title: Improve security posture by eliminating some 3rd party GH actions and pinning others
items.author: lemmy
items.description: Context: https://www.paloaltonetworks.com/blog/cloud-security/trivy-supply-chain-attack/

items.title: SANY: Only lint after semantic analysis and level checking
items.author: ahelwer
items.description:
Fixes crash if linter is run after skipping semantic analysis

Before these changes, SANY would crash if semantic analysis was skipped but linting was run anyway. Now SANY only runs the linter if semantic analysis completed successfully. In addition, SANY requires level-checking to have been run before running the linter, in anticipation of possible future linter messages that might require use of level information; it also just makes sense to have the most "optional" analysis level only run after all the strictly required analysis levels have run.

[Enhancement][Bug][SANY]

items.title: Defer raising TLC_CONFIG_NO_SPEC_BUT_PROPERTY or TLC_CONFIG_NO_FAIRNESS_BUT_LIVE_PROPERTY until Liveness has processed the PROPERTIES and TLC can decide if violations are safety violations.
items.author: lemmy
items.description:
Defer raising TLC_CONFIG_NO_SPEC_BUT_PROPERTY or TLC_CONFIG_NO_FAIRNESS_BUT_LIVE_PROPERTY until Liveness has processed the PROPERTIES and TLC can decide if violations are safety violations.

As a result, TLC no longer raises the warning when PROPERTIES violations are safety violations, i.e., finite prefixes caught by the enhancements in commits b041671 and 01cac36.

Related to Github issue #710
https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/issues/710
Related to Github pull request #1348
https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/pull/1348

[Refactor][TLC]

items.title: Implement lazy evaluation for operator arguments in Liveness.java
items.author: lemmy
items.description:
Implement lazy evaluation for operator arguments in Liveness.java add regression tests for GitHub issue #1161.

Updated Liveness.java to use lazy evaluation via getVal for operator arguments, improving handling of state-level expressions. Added two regression tests (Github1161aTest and Github1161bTest) to verify properties defined through parameterized operators in an INSTANCEd module, along with corresponding TLA+ specifications and configuration files.

Addresses Github issue #1161
https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/issues/1161

[Enhancement][Test]

items.title: TLC reports bogus safety counter-example when checking liveness.
items.author: lemmy
items.description:
When liveness checking encounters an accepting tableau node (sink) during the safety-checking phase of model-checking, TLC used to report a safety violation (finite-prefix counter-example) immediately, without checking the negated property's PossibleErrorModel (PEM). The negated formula can have both a tableau part and PEM conditions (e.g. []<> from fairness). If PEM is non-empty, the finite prefix is a valid counter-example only when those conditions hold on the implied tail (e.g. stuttering). Ignoring PEM caused TLC to report a bogus safety violation for specs where the property actually holds (e.g. under fairness).
Fix: Only report a safety violation when the tableau node is accepting and the PEM is empty (hasEmptyPEM()). Then the entire negated formula is encoded in the tableau and no extra conditions need to hold on the tail, so the finite prefix is a valid counter-example.

Impl:
* Add OrderOfSolution#hasEmptyPEM() and guard the isAccepting() short-circuit in LiveCheck with it.
* Add PossibleErrorModel#isEmpty() and #isSatisfiedByStuttering() (for evaluating PEM on a stuttering tail; used by tests/docs).
* Add documentation that PEM can be treated as an optimization not discussed in the M&P book.

Tests:
* Add tests from GitHub issues #1037 and #790.
* Restore CodePlexBug08AgentRing assertions to their state before the commit that introduced the regression (By chance, this was a valid safety violation).
* Add CodePlexBug08AgentRing790Test, a variant of a test from CodePlexBug08AgentRing where the bug causes TLC to report a bogus counterexample.
* Add test 710c (variant of 710a with fairness): with fairness, TLC reports a valid liveness violation instead of a safety one.
* Add Github710d / Github710dFair and Github710e / Github710eFair: same property, with and without fairness; illustrate safety vs liveness violation depending on spec

Checking OOS#isPEMSatisfiedByStuttering instead of OOS#hasEmptyPEM in addition to `isAccepting` would allow TLC to detect additional safety violations, rather than reporting the (less comprehensive) liveness violation. However, the runtime overhead of this additional check appears too high relative to the limited number of extra inputs it identifies.
Inputs Github710d and Github710dFair illustrate this behavior. TLC reports a safety violation for these inputs iff the enablement conditions of the next-state relation cause stuttering after the final state of the safety violation. If the enablement condition is relaxed, for example, by changing `x < 2` to `x <= 2` or `x < 3` to `x <= 3`, TLC instead reports a liveness violation.

Regression of Github PR #710
https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/issues/710

Fixes Github issues #790 and #1037
https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/issues/790
https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/issues/1037

[Bug][TLC]

items.title: Integrate Formatter into tla2tools
items.author: FedericoPonzi
items.description:
This PR ports the formatter from the [tlaplus-formatter](https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus-formatter/) repo to make it available as part of tlatools.jar; alongside SANY, TLC and other tools. As part of the porting, I've dropped the web related parts.

I created separate commits to ease the review:
1. Initial porting from tlaplus-formatter repo
2. Refactorings required to remove extra dependencies
3. Changes to ant build to include the formatter.
4. Update tests to work on Windows 
5. Update CI to fetch dependencies and run the tests.

Ref: https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus-formatter/issues/22

### Usage

```bash
java -cp tla2tools.jar formatter.Main [OPTIONS] <FILE> [OUTFILE]

Options:
  -i, --indent <N>             Indentation spaces (default: 4)
  -w, --width <N>              Target line width (default: 80)
  -v, --verbosity <LEVEL>      ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG
      --skip-ast-verification  Skip AST verification after formatting
  -h, --help                   Show help message and exit
```

---

### Refactorings implemented to remove extra dependencies:
| Dependency | Reason | Replacement |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| `tla2tools` | Now in same project | Direct classpath resolution |
| `commons-io` | Only used `WriterOutputStream` | `java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream` in `SANYWrapper.java` |
| `commons-cli` | CLI argument parsing | Manual argument parsing in `Main.java` |
| `slf4j` + `logback` | Logging framework | `java.util.logging` in `Main.java`, `TLAPlusFormatter.java`, `TlaDocBuilder.java` |
| JUnit 5 | Test framework | JUnit 4 (already present in tlatools) |

----
### Testing & CI
- **170 formatter unit tests** run on Ubuntu, Windows, and macOS
- **21 TLAPS-dependent tests** (ProofConstructTest, PickStepConstructTest, ExtendsConstructTest) require tlapm + CommunityModules
- **333 semantic preservation tests** run against [tlaplus/examples](https://github.com/tlaplus/examples) (Linux only, in the examples-tests job). These are running the formatter on all spec twice, and ensure AST is preserved.
- Formatter tests are **isolated**: `TLA-Library` is only set for the formatter's `<junit>` block to avoid module shadowing (e.g., `BagsTest` which would break if tlapm's `Bags.tla` shadows the standard module).
- TLA-Library paths are passed as separate Ant properties (`formatter.tlapm.path`, `formatter.community.modules.path`) rather than a single combined value, because Windows `ant.bat` splits on `;` in command-line arguments, silently dropping the second path.

https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/issues/997

[Refactor][TLC]

items.title: Add -suppressMessages and -warningsAsErrors CLI flags to SANY
items.author: craft095
items.description:
Add -suppressMessages and -warningsAsErrors CLI flags to SANY (phase 1 of #1186).
    
Support of the fine-grained warning control in SANY:

-suppressMessages comma-separated integer codes; silences those messages entirely (not
  printed, not a failure).
-warningsAsErrors comma-separated integer warning codes; when any such warning is
  triggered SANY exits with the semantic-analysis failure code.

Both flags validate each supplied code against ErrorCode.fromStandardValue() and reject
unknown codes with a descriptive error. -warningsAsErrors additionally rejects
non-warning-level codes.

This also
- resolves the long-standing TODO comment in frontEndSemanticAnalysis() to split
  errors and warnings into appropriate log levels.
- fixes a bug where linting warnings were accumulated in spec.semanticErrors
  during frontEndLinting() but never printed to the SanyOutput, so they were
  invisible to callers using the API path.

items.title: Enhance CI workflows to support Windows environment.

Updated both main.yml and pr.yml to include 'windows-latest' in the operating system matrix. Adjusted build steps to conditionally run based on the operating system.

[Build][CI]

items.title: Add tests for ENABLED soundness bug with INSTANCE substitution and Java
items.author: lemmy
items.description:
operator overrides.

When a Java-overridden operator (e.g., SelectSeq) receives a LAMBDA that references a primed variable through an INSTANCE substitution, TLC's ENABLED evaluation can silently return the wrong boolean. The root cause is that Context.branch in the OPCODE_enabled handler places a BRANCH node above the INSTANCE substitution bindings. With cutoff=true, getPrimedVar cannot traverse past the BRANCH, so it returns the inner module's SymbolNode instead of resolving through the substitution to the outer variable. TLCStateFun then binds values under inner variable names. If an inner variable name collides with the outer variable name that a different inner variable maps to, TLCStateFun.lookup (which compares by UniqueString) returns the wrong value to the LAMBDA.

Related to Github issue #725
https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/issues/725

[Test][TLC]

items.author: lemmy
items.description:
The TLA+ Toolbox is no longer actively maintained. Add a notice to the welcome page directing users to the actively maintained alternatives: the TLA+ extension for Visual Studio Code and Cursor (which includes a TLA+ debugger), and running TLC from the command line. The notice includes links to the VS Code Marketplace, Open VSX Registry, the tlaplus repository, and a migration guide.

Part of Github issue #1337
https://github.com/tlaplus/tlaplus/issues/1337

[Feature][Doc][Toolbox]

items.title: XMLExporter: don't dump exception on parse failure
items.author: ahelwer
items.description:
Standardized exit codes and modularized exporter functions

Before these changes, the XMLExporter would dump an ugly exception to the console when parsing the spec failed. Since the exporter will be used from TLAPM as its main parser, this is a poor user experience. These changes result in XMLExporter only printing SANY's user-friendly parse error output to the console instead. To support these changes, XMLExporter's possible exit codes were enumerated and the class was slightly broken up to separate the parsing logic from the XML export logic. Tests were added to codify the behavior at various levels.

items.title: XmlExporter: enumerate built-in operator names in schema file
items.author: ahelwer
items.description:
The first commit in this PR also runs the exporter on all tlaplus/examples specs, which is a good test to see whether schema modifications introduce errors. The XML output is, of course, sent to /dev/null. stderr output will be maintained, though.

Also added a bunch of documentation about built-in operators to the schema file.

Ref #1313

items.title: tlaplus-formatter is now included in tlatools
items.author: FedericoPonzi

items.title: fix: honor explicit cfgFile parameter in MCP TLC tools
items.author: lemmy
items.description:
When cfgFile was passed to `tlaplus_mcp_tlc_smoke`, `_check`, or `_explore`, `runTLCInMCP` still ran naming-convention discovery via `getSpecFiles` and returned a "No .cfg found" error if it failed — ignoring the explicit path entirely. Skip discovery when cfgFile is provided and construct SpecFiles directly from the caller-supplied paths.

This supports the standard TLC pattern of multiple .cfg files per module (e.g. positive and negative test configs).

**Reproducer**

Directory contents:

* `MySpec.tla`
* `MySpec_something.cfg`

Not present:

* `MySpec.cfg`
* `MCMySpec.cfg`
* `MCMySpec.tla`

## 0.56.1 - 2026-03-26

### Features

### Bug fixes

items.title: Add `leadsTo` conversion and fix bug in `setBy` transpilation
items.author: bugarela
items.description:
Hello :octocat: 

This adds conversion for `leadsTo` from Quint (which adds this operator in https://github.com/informalsystems/quint/pull/1932) and fixes a bug related to LET IN that I found by getting the following error on a specific `setBy` expression: 

```
The exception was a java.lang.RuntimeException
: Attempted to evaluate an expression of form P /\ Q when P was
a function  of the form (d1 :> e1 @@ ... @@ dN :> eN).
```

I noticed we do caching of the value in `setBy` via a LET IN, which makes a lot of sense. However, that LET IN, when used in an expression inside a conjunction, was being printed like `LET def IN expr /\ foo`, which TLC parses as `LET def IN (expr /\ foo)` which is a problem in this scenario:
```
`f' = LET c == f IN [...] /\ g' = g
```
getting parsed as
```
f' = LET c == f IN ([...] /\ g' = g)
```

which is similar to what originated from the Quint expression I had in my spec:
```
  action SendMsg(i) = all {
    active.get(i),
    nondet j = Nodes.exclude(Set(i)).oneOf()
    all {
      active' = active.set(j, true),
      color' = color.setBy(i, (old => if (j > i) "black" else old)),
      tpos' = tpos,
      tcolor' = tcolor,
    }
  }
```

My suggested fix here is to change both printers to include parenthesis around the LET IN expressions.

<!-- Please ensure that your PR includes the following, as needed -->

- [X] Tests added for any new code
- [X] Ran `make fmt-fix` (or had formatting run automatically on all files edited)
- [ ] Documentation added for any new functionality
- [X] [Entries added to `./unreleased/`][changelog format] for any new functionality

[changelog format]: https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#how-to-record-a-change

# Release notes



## 0.56.0 - 2026-03-20

### Features

- Upgrade Jetty from 11 to 12.1.7 (EE10) and align Jakarta Servlet API to 6.0
- Add gzip and Zstandard (zstd) compression support to the JSON-RPC server. Upgrade Jetty from 12.0.21 to 12.1.7 and use the new CompressionHandler for transparent HTTP content negotiation via Accept-Encoding / Content-Encoding (#3290).
- Add `STATE` query kind to the JSON-RPC `query` method. When `kinds` includes `"STATE"`, the result contains a `state` field with only the last state of the decoded trace (a single ITF state object), instead of the entire trace. This significantly reduces JSON payload size for long symbolic explorations #3288.

items.title: JSON-RPC server: add applyInOrder
items.author: konnov
items.description:
Usually running a single step with the exploration server requires several calls to the server such as `assumeTransition`, `nextStep`, `assumeState`, `checkInvariants`, etc. Instead of doing round-trip every time, we group these calls into a single one, which is executed in a loop on the server.

The initial implementation and documentation was written by Codex GPT 5.4 and refactored by Claude Code Opus 4.6. I only prompted them and pointed to a few inconsistencies.

items.title: Remove Nix from macOS integration-tests path; run directly
items.author: Copilot
items.description:
macOS CI runners OOM during `nix develop` when building `python3.9-certifi-2023.11.17.drv`. Instead of tuning Nix resource limits, this completely removes the Nix path for macOS and installs the required tools directly.

## Changes

The `integration-tests` job is restructured into two conditional paths:

**Linux** (unchanged) — Nix-based path:
- Cache nix store → Install Nix → Build dev-shell → `nix develop -c make integration`

**macOS** (new direct path, no Nix):
- `actions/setup-java@v3` (temurin:17) — JDK 17
- `coursier/setup-action@v1` + `sbt/setup-sbt@v1` — sbt
- `ocaml/setup-ocaml@v3` (OCaml 4.14.x) + `opam install mdx.2.1.0` — provides the `ocaml-mdx` binary used by `test/mdx-test.py`, pinned to 2.1.0 to match `flake.nix` (newer versions have broken ellipsis matching per [mdx#428](https://github.com/realworldocaml/mdx/issues/428))
- Python venv (`python3 -m venv .venv`) with `requests` installed into it — Python dependency used in tests
- `eval $(opam env) && source .venv/bin/activate && source .envrc && make integration` — runs tests directly without Nix

The `source .envrc` replicates what the Nix shellHook previously did: sets `TARGET_DIR`, `APALACHE_HOME`, and adds `bin/` to `PATH`. A Python virtual environment is used instead of a bare `pip3 install` to avoid conflicts with system-managed Python on macOS. `.venv/` is added to `.gitignore`.

---

- [ ] Tests added for any new code
- [ ] Ran `make fmt-fix` (or had formatting run automatically on all files edited)
- [ ] Documentation added for any new functionality
- [ ] [Entries added to `./unreleased/`][changelog format] for any new functionality

> 
> Specifically:

> ```

> 2. For 'Build dev-shell' and 'Build and run integration tests' steps (the steps that run `nix develop` and `nix develop -c make integration`), set environment variables to further limit resources:
>

> 
> Add `env` blocks as shown above to these steps if running on macOS.

>       build-cores = 1

items.title: Fix a bug in Type1Lexer
items.author: konnov
items.description:
We have hit a surprising corner case. A variant tag like `RecvNotification(...)` is not parsed, as the lexer tokenizes it as `Rec`, `vNotification`, etc. Parser combinators are fun to write, but then... I would prefer if we had a generated lexer instead.

- [x] Tests added for any new code
- [x] Ran `make fmt-fix` (or had formatting run automatically on all files edited)
- [x] [Entries added to `./unreleased/`][changelog format] for any new functionality

[changelog format]: https://github.com/apalache-mc/apalache/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#how-to-record-a-change

items.title: v0.56.1
items.author: coffeeinprogress
items.description:
## 0.56.1 - 2026-03-26

### Features

- Add support for the `leadsTo` operator when translating Quint to TLA+

### Bug fixes

- Fix TLA+ printers to wrap LET-IN expressions in parentheses when nested inside another expression, preventing unintended scope extension through `/\` conjuncts
- Fix "used before it is assigned" error when using grouped-variable UNCHANGED with nested operator references (e.g., `vars == <<myList1, myList2>>` where `myList1 == <<myVar1, myVar2>>`), see #3143

items.title: v0.56.0
items.author: coffeeinprogress
items.description:
## 0.56.0 - 2026-03-20

### Features

- Upgrade Jetty from 11 to 12.1.7 (EE10) and align Jakarta Servlet API to 6.0
- Add gzip and Zstandard (zstd) compression support to the JSON-RPC server. Upgrade Jetty from 12.0.21 to 12.1.7 and use the new CompressionHandler for transparent HTTP content negotiation via Accept-Encoding / Content-Encoding (#3290).
- Add `STATE` query kind to the JSON-RPC `query` method. When `kinds` includes `"STATE"`, the result contains a `state` field with only the last state of the decoded trace (a single ITF state object), instead of the entire trace. This significantly reduces JSON payload size for long symbolic explorations #3288.

items.title: v0.55.0
items.author: coffeeinprogress
items.description:
## 0.55.0 - 2026-03-18

### Features

- Add `compact` JSON-RPC method that extracts the last concrete state, reverts to a given snapshot and re-asserts it as a synthetic transition, resetting solver complexity after long symbolic explorations #3285

items.title: v0.52.3
items.author: coffeeinprogress
items.description:
## 0.52.3 - 2026-03-17

### Features

- Add the ordered JSON-RPC method `applyInOrder` for running several stateful exploration operations in one request, together with JSON-RPC documentation for its request and per-step response format (#3280).

### Bug fixes

- Fix Type1Lexer to properly tokenize 'RecvNotification' #3278
